A ONE MILE LONG, 100 YARD WIDE, EF0 TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS OF 85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 61 NORTH IN WILKINSON COUNTY, MS. THE TORNADO WAS RECORDED CROSSING THE HIGHWAY BY A STORM CHASER. SURVEY RESULTS FROM THE EMERGENCY MANAGER NOTED TWO MAIN AREAS OF TREE DAMAGE, ALONG THE HIGHWAY AND OFF CARTER LOOP ROAD. THIS TRACK MAY BE EXTENDED AS SATELLITE DATA BECOMES AVAILABLE AND AREAS THAT ARE NOT REACHABLE BY CAR CAN BE VIEWED.

LIVINGSTING PARISH SHERIFF OFFICE RELAYED A REPORT OF ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME ON THE WEST SIDE OF HIGHWAY 1019 JUST SOUTH OF RAFE BALLARD ROAD. WATER WAS COMING INSIDE THE HOME DUE TO THE ROOF DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS OF 80 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 25 YEARS AND A PATH LENGTH OF 0.122 MILES, TOUCHED DOWN BEHIND A HOUSE AND TORE ITS CAR PORT OFF AND LIFTED MOST OF THE ROOF OFF. IT THEN RIPPED PART OF THE ROOF OFF A NEARBY BUILDING AND PEELED BACK THE TIN ON THE PORCH. TIN WAS PEELED BACK ON A BUILDING ACROSS THE STREET. ALL DEBRIS WAS THROWN AROUND A QUARTER OF A MILE TO THE NORTHEAST.

UPDATED THE PATH LENGTH DUE TO ADDITIONAL DAMAGE INFORMATION. A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A FEW LARGE TREES ROUGHLY SNAPPED NEAR THE BASE AND LEANING INTO HOUSES, FROM EAST TO WEST. TWO MORE TREES WITH SHALLOW ROOTS WERE ALSO UPROOTED. THE NEIGHBORHOOD HAD WIDESPREAD SMALL LIMBS DOWN AND TREE DEBRIS. BASED ON THE SURVEY AND RADAR IMAGERY AT THE TIME, THIS SEEMS TO BE THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH A DECAYING MESOSCALE CONVECTIVE VORTEX (MCV), OR A LARGE AREA OF STRONG WINDS. THE INITIAL ESTIMATE OF THE WIND SPEED WAS LIKELY 75-85 MPH. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON EYE WITNESS REPORTS AND RADAR. MORE ANALYSIS WILL BE DONE AND THIS COULD BE UPDATED IN THE NEXT FEW DAYS.

A TORNADO MOVED ONSHORE NEAR GUSTE ISLAND IN MADISONVILLE AND TRACKED N-NW ACROSS MAINLY UNINHABITED SWAMP LAND. SPORADIC SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ON THE EASTERN SHORE OF AN INLET. EF-0 WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS AROUND 75 MPH.

AN EF0 TORNADO, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS, A PATH LENGTH OF 0.3 MILES AND MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH, TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE RIVER AND SOUTH OF THE AIRPORT IN KENNER AND TOOK THE ROOF OFF A LARGE BUILDING. IT WAS NOTED THE ROOF MATERIAL WAS SEVERELY CORRODED AND RUSTED. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTH AND CONVERGENCE IN DOWNED TREES WAS SEEN JUST TO THE EAST OF A CEMETERY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO TRACK NORTH-NORTHEAST, WITH TREES DOWN NEAR THE RAILROAD TRACKS AND POWER LINES DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE TRACKS, WHERE IT LOOKS LIKE THE TORNADO LIFTED. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY ONE MINUTE.

THE EF0 70 MPH TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR DELLWOOD DRIVE AND WESTWOOD DRIVE, WHERE A 12 TREE TOP WAS BROKEN OFF THE MAIN TRUNK. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST TO NORTHWOOD AVE WHERE IT KNOCKED AN ENTIRE SECTION OF A WOODEN PRIVACY FENCE DOWN AND BROKE A FEW 8-10 BRANCHES AND DAMAGED THE ROOF OF A DETACHED GARAGE. CONTINUING NNEWARD, THERE WERE SMALL BRANCHES DOWN ALONG WOODHAVEN AVE AND THE FINAL AREA OF DAMAGE WAS A 16-18 TREE TOP BROKEN/TWISTED IN THE 1800TH BLOCK OF 22ND ST, AND A FEW ADDITIONAL SMALLER TREETOPS BEHIND THESE HOUSES. LASTLY, A LARGE TREE (BASE 28 INCHES X 22 INCHES) ON THE CORNER OF 22ND ST AND INGALLS AVE WAS PARTIALLY UPROOTED AND WAS LEANING TOWARD THE NORTH AT ABOUT A 20 DEGREE ANGLE.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO. A LIKELY WATERSPOUT MOVED ONSHORE NEAR RAILROAD ST AND HENDERSON AVE WHERE IT CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOUSES. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR WITMAN AVE WHERE A HOUSE SUSTAINED EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO TRACKED TO THE NORTHWEST, CAUSING ADDITIONAL MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND SNAPPED TREES NEAR BLUE LAKE COVE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO MOVE NORTHWEST WHERE A ROOF WAS DAMAGED NEAR PRENTISS RD AND HILLCREST DRIVE. IT THEN MOVED OFFSHORE INTO THE BAY OF ST LOUIS WHERE IT CONTINUED ON ITS NORTHWESTWARD TRACK. AFTER CROSSING THE BAY, IT TOUCHED DOWN ONSHORE ONCE AGAIN, CROSSING INTO DIAMONDHEAD. IT CROSSED I- 10 NEAR DIAMONDHEAD DRIVE N WHERE IT DAMAGED AND PEELED SHINGLES OFF OF THE MEMORIAL WOODLAND VILLAGE REHABILITATION AND NURSING CENTER THEN LIKELY LIFTED SHORTLY AFTERWARDS WEST OF GEX RD AND LEISURE TIME DR.

A WATERSPOUT LIKELY MOVED ONSHORE NEAR THE GREAT SOUTHERN GOLF CLUB DURING THE MORNING OF AUGUST 30. SEVERAL SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES WERE SEEN FROM NOAA AERIAL IMAGERY IN THE GULF CLUB AND IN A WOODED AREA TO THE NORTHEAST OF THE CLUB. THE TORNADO TRACKED TO THE NORTH-NORTHEAST, CAUSING DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG PALMER DRIVE NEAR MAGAZINE CIRCLE, OAKRIDGE CIRCLE, HAYDEN DRIVE, AND LIPSCOMB COURT. A PATH OF DOWNED TREES WAS NOTED JUST NORTH OF OAKRIDGE CIRCLE. ROOF PANELS WERE MISSING FROM A STORAGE FACILITY BUILDING ON E PASS RD. THE TORNADO LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER, BUT NOT BEFORE CROSSING DEBUYS ROAD AND RIPPING SIDING OFF A HOUSE ON RAINBOW DRIVE. A FENCE, TREES, AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE ALSO DOWN ON RAINBOW DRIVE.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE EDGEWATER MALL NEAR RUE PETIT BOIS WHERE AN UPROOTED TREE AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED. NUMEROUS SHINGLES WERE MISSING FROM A BANK ON PASS ROAD WHILE A PORTION OF A ROOF WAS TORN OFF A DUNKIN DONUTS. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTH-NORTHEAST AND PRODUCED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND DOWNED TREES ALONG LAKEVIEW DRIVE AND FAIRVIEW DRIVE. SHINGLES WERE MISSING FROM AN APARTMENT COMPLEX ALONG BIG LAKE ROAD. A CONVERGENT PATTERN WAS EVIDENT IN THE TALL MARSHY GRASS ALONG THE SOUTH SHORE OF BIG LAKE PER NOAA AERIAL IMAGERY. THE CIRCULATION CROSSED BIG LAKE AND CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES NEAR THE SUNKIST COUNTRY CLUB, INCLUDING ALONG BARRETT ROAD, RUE MAISON, LACKLAND DRIVE, AND CLUB MOSS DRIVE.

A TORNADO SPAWNED FROM HURRICANE IDA JUST NORTH OF E RAILROAD ST. AND CONTINUED NORTHWEST PRODUCING SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE. MOST NOTABLE DAMAGE OCCURRED ON 27TH ST. WHERE NUMEROUS LARGE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED AND A LARGE OAK TREE WAS UPROOTED ONTO TWO HOUSES. A HOUSE LOST PART OF ITS ROOF ON BROADMOORE PL. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED NORTH OF PASS RD.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EDEN ISLE JUST EAST OF I-10. THE FIRST SIGN OF VISIBLE DAMAGE WAS TO A VACANT CONDOMINIUM COMPLEX ON LAKE SHORE BLVD EAST. THE ROOF TO TWO OF THE BUILDINGS WERE PARTIALLY TORN WITH CONSTRUCTION DEBRIS AND DOWNED TREES BLOWN TO THE WEST. THE TORNADO TRACKED TO THE WEST-NORTHWEST, PASSING ACROSS I-10 AND CAUSING DOWNED TREES AND PROPERTY DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS HOMES ALONG LAKESHORE BLVD S, CUTTYSARK CV, ROYAL PALMS, MOONRAKER DR, PONTCHARTRAIN DR, CLARA DR, AND CARR DR.

A WATERSPOUT OVER HERON BAY MOVED INLAND ACROSS HANCOCK COUNTY MARSH COASTAL PRESERVE. NUMEROUS ROAD SIGNS AND POSTS WERE BLOWN OVER IN THE MAGNOLIA RIDGE AREA. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHWEST ACROSS EASTERN AND NORTHERN PEARLINGTON. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND LARGE BRANCHES SNAPPED ON DEAN ROAD, WHIPPLE ROAD, RUSS STREET AND NAPOLEON-WESTONIA ROAD. ROOF DAMAGE TO MULTIPLE ROOF HOMES ON DEAN ROAD WAS REPORTED. THE BEGINNING AND ENDING POINTS OF THE TRACK ARE UNKNOWN WITHOUT THE ABILITY TO PERFORM A GROUND OR AERIAL SURVEY IN THE WAKE OF HURRICANE IDA.

A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EDEN ISLE ON MARINA DR WHERE SEVERAL HOUSES SUSTAINED ROOF AND FENCE DAMAGE. UPROOTED TREES AND ADDITIONAL PROPERTY DAMAGE WERE CAPTURED BY NOAA AERIAL IMAGERY IN THE TORNADO S PATH ALONG CONSTELLATION DR, CHARLES CT, AND INTREPID DR, AND EDEN ISLES DR. THE TORNADO MAY HAVE CONTINUED BEYOND THIS POINT, BUT AERIAL IMAGERY WAS UNAVAILABLE TO CONFIRM THIS.

A NWS TEAM CONDUCTED REMOTE STORM SURVEYS VIA DAMAGE REPORTS FROM EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND HIGH-RESOLUTION AERIAL PHOTOGRAPHY IMAGES FROM THE NOAA REMOTE SENSING DIVISION FOLLOWING HURRICANE IDA. THE TEAM FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF- 0 TORNADO IN WAVELAND, MS. AFTER MOVING ONSHORE IN A NORTHWEST DIRECTION, THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN ON LAND NEAR MORRIS ST., AMAR ST., AND SINGLETON ST., DAMAGING ROOFS TO 8-10 HOUSES IN THIS NEIGHBORHOOD AND SNAPPING OR UPROOTING SEVERAL TREES. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED NORTHWEST, DAMAGING ROOFS TO 4-5 HOUSES ON WAVELAND AVE. AND SYCAMORE ST. THERE WAS EVIDENCE OF ROOF DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS APARTMENTS IN THE OAK PLACE APARTMENTS NEAR RT 90 AND AN ADDITIONAL 8-10 HOMES ALONG MEDITERRANEAN ST., PACIFIC ST., AND DANUBE ST. THE TORNADO LIKELY LIFTED JUST SOUTH OF ROUTE 43/KILN RD IN HANCOCK COUNTY, MS.

A NWS TEAM CONDUCTED REMOTE STORM SURVEYS VIA DAMAGE REPORTS FROM EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND HIGH-RESOLUTION AERIAL PHOTOGRAPHY IMAGES FROM THE NOAA REMOTE SENSING DIVISION FOLLOWING HURRICANE IDA. THE TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH ANOTHER EF-0 TORNADO IN WAVELAND, MS. AFTER MOVING ONSHORE, THE FIRST EVIDENCE OF DAMAGE WAS TO A COUPLE OF RESIDENTIAL ROOFS NEAR DANIEL STREET AND JEFF DAVIS AVENUE. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED NORTHWEST ACROSS HIGHWAY 90 BEFORE DAMAGING SEVERAL HOMES AND SNAPPING OR UPROOTING SEVERAL TREES NORTH OF ROUTE 43 BETWEEN 3RD AVE AND D AVE. THE TORNADO LIKELY LIFTED JUST SOUTHEAST OF WHALE AVENUE.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO IN HENDERSON POINT, MS. A LIKELY WATERSPOUT FORMED OVER THE MISSISSIPPI SOUND THEN MOVED ONSHORE NEAR HWY 90 AND FORT HENRY AVE. IT TOUCHED DOWN AS A TORNADO AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO TREES AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO A DETACHED GARAGE OF A HOUSE ON FORT HENRY AVE. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED NORTH-NORTHWEST, CAUSING SEVERAL HOUSES TO TAKE ROOF DAMAGE IN A SUBDIVISION NEAR BAYVIEW ST, ALONG WITH TREES AND POWER LINES SNAPPED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ON ITS NORTH- NORTHWESTWARD TRACK, CAUSING ADDITIONAL DAMAGE TO HOUSES ON LOUISIANA AVE AND DOWNED TREES. IT THEN LIFTED AFTERWARDS, NEAR LIVINGSTON DRIVE.

A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT ONE-QUARTER MILE SOUTHWEST OF KEESLER AIR FORCE BASE IN BILOXI, MS. SEVERAL HOUSES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE ALONG MIRAMAR AVE AND CLOWER ST. LARGE BRANCHES WERE ALSO SNAPPED IN THIS AREA. THE TORNADO LIFTED QUICKLY BUT NOT BEFORE PRODUCING MORE DAMAGE FARTHER NORTHWEST ALONG ITS PATH, INCLUDING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL APARTMENT BUILDINGS ALONG IBERVILLE DR. A FEW HARDWOOD TREES FELL IN A WOODED AREA JUST TO THE WEST OF THE APARTMENT COMPLEX.

A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TRACKED NORTH FOR APPROXIMATELY A HALF MILE IN BILOXI, PRODUCING DOWNED TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES AS WELL AS ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES WERE OBSERVED ALONG HOWARD AVE., PEYTON DR., KELLER AVE., AND ESTERS BLVD. ROOF DAMAGE TO A SMALL OFFICE COMPLEX AT THE CORNER OF DIVISION ST. AND KELLER AVE. AND SEVERAL NEARBY HOUSES WERE ALSO NOTED.

ANOTHER EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN GULFPORT, MS, SNAPPING NUMEROUS TREES AND DAMAGING SEVERAL ROOFS IN THE NEIGHBORHOOD BETWEEN 26TH AND 28TH STREETS AND ROBERTS AND HEWES AVENUES. IT THEN TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR AT LEAST A HALF MILE, PRODUCING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND UPROOTING SEVERAL TREES ON 30TH STREET AND D AVENUE. THE TRACK MAY HAVE CONTINUED BRIEFLY BEYOND THIS POINT, BUT THE HIGH-RESOLUTION AERIAL PHOTOGRAPHY FROM THE NOAA REMOTE SENSING DIVISION FOLLOWING HURRICANE IDA WAS NOT AVAILABLE THIS FAR INLAND TO CONFIRM ADDITIONAL DAMAGE.

SEVERAL SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES WERE SEEN FROM NOAA AERIAL IMAGERY IN THE GULF CLUB AND IN A WOODED AREA TO THE NORTHEAST OF THE CLUB. CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 5 E GULFPORT. A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE EDGEWATER MALL NEAR RUE PETIT BOIS WHERE AN UPROOTED TREE AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED. NUMEROUS SHINGLES WERE MISSING FROM A BANK ON PASS ROAD WHILE A PORTION OF A ROOF WAS TORN OFF A DUNKIN DONUTS. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTH-NORTHEAST AND PRODUCED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND DOWNED TREES ALONG LAKEVIEW DRIVE AND FAIRVIEW DRIVE. SHINGLES WERE MISSING FROM AN APARTMENT COMPLEX ALONG BIG LAKE ROAD. THE CIRCULATION CROSSED BIG LAKE AND CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES NEAR THE SUNKIST COUNTRY CLUB, INCLUDING ALONG BARRETT ROAD, RUE MAISON, LACKLAND DRIVE, AND CLUB MOSS DRIVE.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE MISSISSIPPI COAST COLISEUM. A HALF DOZEN HOUSES IN A COMMUNITY ALONG PINEWOOD DR AND ARBOR DR SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. AN AIR CONDITIONER WAS BLOWN OFF ITS PAD WHILE SEVERAL TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTH ACROSS PASS RD WHERE A PORTION OF A RESTAURANT AND BANK WERE TORN OFF AND SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWN IN AN ADJOINING PARKING LOT. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED TO THE NORTH-NORTHWEST, EVENTUALLY CROSSING POPPS FERRY RD TOWARD CARTER RD, MARTIN RD, AND OLD BAY RD. AERIAL IMAGERY FROM NOAA SHOWED MISSING SHINGLES TO NUMEROUS HOUSES, DOWNED FENCES AND A FEW DOWNED TREES IN THE TORNADO S PATH. THE TORNADO TRACKED ALONG CARMARGUE LN AND EITHER DISSIPATED SHORTLY THEREAFTER OR CONTINUED AS WATERSPOUT IN BIG LAKE.

A TORNADO, LIKELY ORIGINATING AS A WATERSPOUT CAME ONSHORE ACROSS PORTIONS OF SOUTHWESTERN PASCAGOULA SOUTH OF INGALLS AVENUE. DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED AS LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN ON PROPERTY ON GENERAL LEE ST. THE WEAK TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHWEST CROSSING US 90 CAUSING DAMAGE TO A HOME ALONG AMONETT STREET. MORE CLUSTERED DAMAGE WAS REPORTED ACROSS MOSS POINT AS THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHWEST WHICH CONSISTED OF HOMES WITH ROOFING, FENCE, SIDING AND WINDOW DAMAGE. THE TORNADO CROSSED US63 AND EVENTUALLY INTERSTATE 10 BEFORE CAUSING MORE LIGHT ROOFING DAMAGE TO HOMES ALONG CODA ROAD. THE TORNADO LIKELY DISSIPATED THEREAFTER NORTH OF POPPY DRIVE.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO IN MANDEVILLE, LA. THE TORNADO MOVED ONSHORE FROM LAKE PONTCHARTRAIN AND TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF MARINERS BLVD NEAR N CAUSEWAY BLVD, CAUSING MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO THE TIN ROOFING MATERIAL OFF OF A RESIDENTIAL BUILDING. IT THEN TRACKED NORTHWARD, DAMAGING A SMALL TREE AND RIPPING OFF MORE SMALL SECTIONS OF TIN ROOFING MATERIAL ON MARINERS PLAZA. IT THEN PROGRESSED NORTHWARD, LIFTING JUST BEFORE REACHING THE INTERSECTION OF E CAUSEWAY APPROACH AND MONROE ST.
CAUSEWAY/MANDEVILLE P.D. REPORTS POSSIBLE WATERSPOUT VIA VIDEO LOCATED NEAR 100 BLOCK OF N CAUSEWAY.

MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ALONG THE HIGHWAY AND IN OTHER AREAS AROUND A TORNADO TRACK DUE TO STRAIGHT LINE DAMAGING WINDS.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF BALES AVE IN PICAYUNE, MS. THIS TORNADO QUICKLY TRAVELED NORTHEAST, WITH WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE IN THE EASTERN PART OF THE TOWN. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASE WITH MULTIPLE TIN ROOFS RIPPED OFF AS WELL AS MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS TO TWO TRAILERS JUST WEST OF THE HIGHWAY. ONE WAS SPUN 180 DEGREES AND FLIPPED WITH EXTENSIVE DAMAGE AND ANOTHER ROLLED 100 YARDS AND WAS COMPLETELY OBLITERATED. THE TORNADO CROSSED THE HIGHWAY WITH OTHER STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE NOTED ALONG THE DAMAGE PATH ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE HIGHWAY. THE TORNADO SEEMS TO HAVE LIFTED NEAR NORTHWOOD DRIVE WHERE A SMALL SHED WAS DESTROYED ALONG WITH MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AND MULTIPLE TREES AND BRANCHES SNAPPED. ADDITIONAL DATA MAY BECOME AVAILABLE WHEN CONDITIONS ARE BETTER FOR A DRONE TO TAKE FLIGHT. ON BOTH SIDES OF THE PATH THERE WAS EVIDENCE OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WHICH WAS CONSISTENT WITH RADAR AT THE TIME. THE WIDTH THAT WAS ESTIMATED WAS DUE TO A WIDER SWATH SEEN IN THE EAST PART OF THE CITY. IT WAS DIFFICULT TO DISTINGUISH TORNADO VS. STRAIGHT LINE WIND AT TIMES. THIS LSR IS THE END POINT OF THE TORNADO. START: 30:40, -88.5177 END: 30.4114, -88.5033.

*** 1 FATAL *** AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BILLY AND 1ST STREETS. IT PROCEEDED NORTHEAST WHERE IT REMOVED A LARGE SECTION OF THE ROOF OF A WELL BUILT HOME. IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST WHERE IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 63 AND INTERACTED WITH A SOUTHBOUND VEHICLE. THE DRIVER WAS KILLED WHEN THEY WERE EJECTED AFTER LOSING CONTROL OF THEIR VEHICLE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST WHERE IT DID MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES AND TREES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR MARY AVENUE. MAX WINDS WERE 95 MPH.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HWY 190 AND HOLLYCREST BLVD IN COVINGTON WHERE DAMAGE WAS FIRST NOTED IN THE HOLLYCREST PLAZA SHOPPING MALL. A PORTION OF THE NORTH-FACING EXTERIOR WALL OF THE BUILDING COMPLEX COLLAPSED. THERE WAS ALSO MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND THE FENCE IN THE BACK PARKING LOT WAS BLOWN OVER. SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE (SMALL PINES UPROOTED AND LARGE BRANCHES SNAPPED) WAS NOTED ALONG 5TH AVE, SOELL DR, AND MIRE DR AS THE TORNADO TRACKED TO THE EAST.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM HAS DETERMINED THAT THE DAMAGE IN METAIRIE, LA WAS CAUSED BY STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS. THERE WERE TWO AREAS OF DAMAGE SURVEYED. ONE WAS ON CLEARVIEW PKWAY AND ONE ON HARING ROAD. THE FIRST AREA HAD ONE HOME WITH SHINGLE DAMAGE, A DAMAGED CARPORT AND A FRONT PILLAR SHIFTED OFF THE PORCH. TWO NEIGHBORING HOUSES HAD ABOUT 5-10 FEET OF SHINGLE/AWNING DAMAGE. SURROUNDING THESE HOUSES, THERE WAS LITTLE TO NO LEAF OR TREE DEBRIS, NO OTHER STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED, CHRISTMAS DECORATIONS WERE NOT MOVED AND THE TARPED ROOFS WERE NOT TOUCHED. IN THE SECOND LOCATION, ONLY ONE HOUSE HAD A FEW FEET OF SIDING DAMAGE AND MANY OF THE CHRISTMAS DECORATIONS WERE NOT MOVED WHILE A FEW WERE. THERE WAS SOME LEAF DEBRIS NOTED IN THIS AREA. IT IS ALSO IMPORTANT TO NOTE THAT THESE AREAS DID SUSTAIN WIDESPREAD MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE FROM HURRICANE IDA. IN SUMMARY, DUE TO THE VERY ISOLATED DAMAGE AND THE RELATED COMPLICATIONS DUE TO IDA, WE CANNOT CONCLUSIVELY DETERMINE THIS WAS A TORNADO. THIS DAMAGE WILL BE DETERMINED AS 60 MPH STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS UNLESS ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS FOUND.
